cells.name: Basophil
cells.key_features: Moderate number of coarse, blue-black cytoplasmic granules of varying shape and size
cells.nucleus: Segmented nuclei with coarse chromatin, may be partially obscured by granules
cells.clinical_significance: Increased in myeloproliferative neoplasms and chronic myeloid leukemia

cells.name: Eosinophil
cells.key_features: Numerous coarse, bright-orange cytoplasmic granules of uniform size
cells.nucleus: Mature forms typically have 2 nuclear lobes separated by thin chromatin filament
cells.normal_percentage: Low numbers in normal bone marrow
cells.clinical_significance: Increased in drug reactions, allergic reactions, parasite infections, some myeloid and lymphoid malignancies
cells.notes: Eosinophil myelocytes may contain dark-purple granules among eosinophilic granules

cells.name: Mast Cell
cells.key_features: Large cell with abundant cytoplasm packed with purple-black or black coarse granules
cells.nucleus: Round nucleus, often partially obscured by granules
cells.normal_percentage: Low numbers in normal bone marrow
cells.clinical_significance: Mildly increased in allergic reactions, myeloproliferative neoplasms, lymphoproliferative disorders, systemic mastocytosis
cells.notes: Atypical spindle-shaped forms seen in systemic mastocytosis

cells.name: Neutrophil (Segmented or Band)
cells.key_features: Pale-pink cytoplasm with numerous secondary (lilac) granules
cells.nucleus: Band shaped or segmented into 2-5 lobes connected by thin chromatin filaments
cells.normal_percentage: 15% to 30% of nucleated cells in bone marrow
cells.notes: Band nucleus indented more than half diameter of theoretical round nucleus

cells.name: Metamyelocyte
cells.key_features: Abundant secondary (lilac) granules with few residual primary granules
cells.nucleus: Nuclear indentation less than half of theoretical round nucleus, clumped chromatin
cells.normal_percentage: 10% to 20% of nucleated cells in bone marrow
cells.notes: Nuclear indentation >50% indicates maturation to band form

cells.name: Myelocyte
cells.key_features: Round to oval cell with round nucleus often eccentrically located, may be flattened on one side. Contains secondary (lilac) granules plus residual primary (azurophilic) granules
cells.nucleus: Variable chromatin clumping, nucleoli absent, clear region (hof) may be present
cells.normal_percentage: 10% to 15% of nucleated cells in bone marrow
cells.clinical_significance: Increased in left-shifted granulopoiesis, myeloproliferative neoplasms, chronic myeloid leukemia, granulocyte growth factor therapy

cells.name: Promyelocyte
cells.key_features: Generally larger than myeloblast, basophilic cytoplasm with distinct primary (azurophilic) granules visible over nucleus. Lacks secondary (lilac) granules
cells.nucleus: Round to oval with fine, blast-like or slightly clumped chromatin and 1-3 visible nucleoli
cells.normal_percentage: Up to 8% of nucleated cells in bone marrow
cells.clinical_significance: Increased in granulocyte growth factor therapy
cells.notes: Clear region (hof) may be present adjacent to nucleus

cells.name: Myeloblast
cells.key_features: Round to oval cell with scanty, basophilic, agranular cytoplasm. May contain small number of delicate granules
cells.nucleus: Round or slightly irregular with dispersed, finely reticulated chromatin and 1 or more nucleoli
cells.normal_percentage: 0% to 2% of nucleated cells in bone marrow
cells.clinical_significance: Increased in myelodysplastic syndromes and acute myeloid leukemia

cells.name: Erythroid Precursor, Pronormoblast
cells.key_features: Large cell with distinct cytoplasmic border, blue cytoplasm darker than myeloblast but lighter than basophilic normoblast, visible perinuclear hof
cells.nucleus: Large with distinct nuclear membrane, round or slightly oval, 1 or more prominent nucleoli. Finely reticulated, lacy, blast-like chromatin without clumping
cells.normal_percentage: <3% of nucleated erythroid cells in normal bone marrow
cells.notes: Most immature cells in the erythroid series

cells.name: Erythroid Precursor, Basophilic Normoblast
cells.key_features: Smaller than pronormoblast, intensely basophilic cytoplasm
cells.nucleus: Round to slightly oval, chromatin is open but with thicker, more clumped strands than pronormoblast
cells.normal_percentage: 6% to 8% of nucleated erythroid cells in bone marrow

cells.name: Erythroid Precursor, Polychromatophilic Normoblast
cells.key_features: Round cell with polychromatophilic (blue-pink) cytoplasm due to increasing hemoglobin concentration
cells.nucleus: Lacks nucleoli, more condensed chromatin than earlier stages
cells.normal_percentage: 90% of nucleated erythroid cells (combined with orthochromic normoblasts)

cells.name: Erythroid Precursor, Orthochromic Normoblast
cells.key_features: Late-stage erythroid precursor, smaller than polychromatophilic normoblast. Cytoplasm stains uniformly pinkish orange due to hemoglobinization with little or no basophilia
cells.nucleus: Very small, often pyknotic, sometimes appears as homogeneous mass of dense chromatin. Often eccentrically placed and may be extruding or fragmented
cells.normal_percentage: 90% of nucleated erythroid cells (combined with polychromatophilic normoblasts)

cells.name: Monocyte
cells.key_features: Round to oval with abundant gray or gray-blue cytoplasm that may contain vacuoles and/or fine azurophilic granules. Pseudopod-like cytoplasmic extensions may be seen
cells.nucleus: Often indented but may be band-like. Chromatin only slightly clumped, nucleoli seen only rarely
cells.clinical_significance: May be increased in reactive conditions even without peripheral monocytosis

cells.name: Monocyte, Immature (Promonocyte)
cells.key_features: Larger than mature monocyte with convoluted or folded nucleus
cells.nucleus: Fine chromatin pattern, nucleoli frequently seen
cells.notes: Included in blast count when performing manual bone marrow differential

cells.name: Foamy Macrophage
cells.key_features: Round to oval with abundant cytoplasm that appears foamy
cells.nucleus: Similar to normal monocytes or macrophages
cells.clinical_significance: Result from accumulation of sphingomyelin, seen in diseases affecting sphingomyelin metabolism

cells.name: Lymphocyte
cells.key_features: Round to slightly oval mononuclear cell with dense chromatin. Variable amount of cytoplasm ranging from pale to dark blue
cells.nucleus: Dense chromatin, nucleoli absent. Perinuclear clear zone occasionally seen
cells.clinical_significance: Increased in infectious conditions (pertussis, typhoid fever), chronic inflammatory conditions, drug reactions, endocrine disorders
cells.notes: Important to distinguish from malignant lymphocytes and nucleated RBCs

cells.name: Plasma Cell
cells.key_features: Small to medium-sized cell with round nucleus, clock-face chromatin, paranuclear hof, and abundant dark-blue cytoplasm
cells.normal_percentage: Normal in bone marrow, not generally in blood
cells.clinical_significance: Increased in chronic inflammatory conditions, autoimmune disorders, alcoholism, drug reactions, sarcoidosis, granulomatous diseases. Malignant forms in plasma cell neoplasms (myeloma) and lymphomas with plasmacytic differentiation
